channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Times Readers Pick the 100 Greatest Living American Songwriters
item.description: 25,000 people responded to our poll. Here are their top picks.
item.dc:creator: Nitsuh Abebe

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘recant’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 5 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Trump ignoró los simulacros bélicos sobre el estrecho de Ormuz
item.description: Durante las dos últimas décadas, Irán amenazó repetidamente con cerrar la vía marítima. Trump subestimó la capacidad de Irán para hacerlo.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: El control iraní del estrecho de Ormuz se ha convertido en una enorme fuente de influencia en las negociaciones con Donald Trump.

item.title: California Governor’s Race Remains Too Close to Call
item.description: Steve Hilton, a Republican, and Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, were leading in election night returns. Tom Steyer, another Democrat, urged patience as more votes were being counted.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: It’s the first time since California began using the top-two primary system that a governor’s race has been so close.

item.title: Democratic Voters Acted Strategically in a Turbulent California Election
item.description: Without a dominant candidate in the governor’s race, Democratic voters ultimately wanted to ensure that their party wasn’t shut out of the general election.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Minh Connors for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kevin Stull, right, fills out a ballot as barber Bryan Diaz waits to give a haircut at Ana’s Hair Salon on Election Day in the Mission District of San Francisco.

item.title: Rutina de 15 minutos con la cuerda de saltar para principiantes
item.description: Saltar a la cuerda no es solo cosa de niños. Prueba esta rutina para empezar.
item.dc:creator: Connie Chang

media:content.media:credit: Theodore Tae/The New York Times

item.title: Republican support for same-sex marriage has fallen by 18 percentage points since 2022.
item.description: summary
item.dc:creator: Ruth Igielnik
item.category: Same-Sex Marriage, Civil Unions and Domestic Partnerships

media:content.media:credit: Maansi Srivastava/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: New York City Pride March in 2023.

item.title: Trump falló como comandante en jefe
item.description: En tiempos de guerra, los líderes deben unir al país. Trump, en cambio, se ha dedicado a buscar el beneficio personal.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración fotográfica de Naila Ruechel para The New York Times. Fuente de las fotografías para la ilustración: Haiyun Jiang para The New York Times y Getty Images.

item.title: Marni von Wilpert and Jim Desmond Advance in a San Diego-Area House Race
item.description: Ms. von Wilpert’s win in Tuesday’s open primary eliminated Democrats’ fears that two Republicans would advance to November in the 48th Congressional District.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

item.title: Even if Oil Prices Peak Soon, the Global Economy Will Slow This Year
item.description: The Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development, in a report, said the Middle East war’s consequences “are likely to be felt for some time.”
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Oli Scarff/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An oil refinery in England. Higher energy prices because of the war in Iran will slow global economic growth and raise inflation through 2027, the O.E.C.D. said.

item.title: Want Courtside Seats for the Knicks? Be Rich, Famous and a Die-Hard Fan.
item.description: Prime tickets on the floor for the N.B.A. Finals are coveted and very hard to get. The No. 1 requirement: You must love the Knicks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir

media:content.media:credit: Al Bello/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Timothée Chalamet, Kylie Jenner and Ben Stiller are courtside regulars at Knicks games.

item.title: ¿Cuánto tiempo puede seguir así Marco Rubio?
item.description: El secreto del éxito del secretario de Estado de EE. UU.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Roig-Franzia

item.title: George Conway Won Fame as a Never-Trumper. Will New York Voters Buy It?
item.description: Mr. Conway has centered his House campaign on his well-publicized fight against President Trump, arguing that no issue is more important to the nation.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times

item.title: This Rugged New York Heron Is Suddenly Facing Extinction. Why?
item.description: Black-crowned night herons could go the way of the passenger pigeon, vanishing from the city. Why remains a mystery.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: They’re big, and they’re friendly (mostly). But black-crowned night herons and their chicks, pictured above, face perilous times.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 3, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Wants Trump to Give Peace Talks a Chance
item.description: “Oh, I’m sorry, is wrapping up the war you started not interesting enough?” Michael Kosta said on Tuesday, after the president called negotiations with Iran “boring.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Word of the Day: recant
item.description: This word has appeared in five articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Incumbent Karen Bass Advances to L.A. Mayoral Election in November
item.description: Her opponent remains undetermined.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass at the Los Angeles County Democratic Watch Party on Tuesday.

item.title: Wiener and Chan Will Face Off in House Race to Succeed Nancy Pelosi
item.description: State Sen. Scott Wiener and Supervisor Connie Chan were the top two finishers in the open primary to represent San Francisco as Nancy Pelosi retires.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Anastasiia Sapon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: State Sen. Scott Wiener at a campaign event during his run for San Francisco’s main congressional seat.

item.title: I Went to Lebanon and Found Something Strangely Hopeful
item.description: If given the chance, the country could remake itself on its own terms.
item.dc:creator: Lydia Polgreen and William Keo

media:content.media:credit: William Keo for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Es malo dormir con mi mascota?
item.description: Parece que todo el mundo tiene una opinión, así que les planteamos la pregunta a los expertos.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Schupak

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas para The New York Times

item.title: A Trump Endorsement Falls Flat: 4 Election Takeaways From Iowa and Beyond
item.description: The president’s pick for governor of Iowa lost his primary, while Democrats in the state chose their nominee in what they hope will be a competitive Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Scott Olson/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Representative Randy Feenstra, a Republican, lost his primary bid for governor of Iowa despite a late endorsement from President Trump.

item.title: Zach Lahn Wins Republican Primary for Iowa Governor
item.description: Zach Lahn’s victory was a rare case this year in which a candidate defeated an opponent backed by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman and Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: KC McGinnis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Zach Lahn at an event in Clive, Iowa, in May.

item.title: Representative Brad Sherman to Face Larry Thompson in November
item.description: The 15th-term congressman will face a Republican film producer after they both advanced in a nonpartisan House primary in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

item.title: Gregg Hull Wins G.O.P. Nomination to Be New Mexico Governor
item.description: Gregg Hull, the former mayor of Rio Rancho, a suburb of Albuquerque, will try to break the Democrats’ hold on the southwestern state.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault

media:content.media:credit: Morgan Lee/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Gregg Hull was one of three Republican candidates seeking the nomination for governor of New Mexico.

item.title: Becerra’s Heritage Appeals to Many Latino Voters, but Some Shrug
item.description: If Xavier Becerra wins the primary, he has a strong chance of becoming California’s first Latino governor since 1875. That’s a draw for some Latino voters, but other factors are driving their votes.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Xavier Becerra attended a phone banking event at the SEIU 2015 offices on Saturday.

item.title: For Dubai’s Migrants, War Is One More Worry in Super Stressed Lives
item.description: For laborers in Dubai, a free stress-management class offers a temporary oasis of calm amid struggles with debt, loneliness, long hours and, in recent months, the fear of missile strikes.
item.dc:creator: Mansi Choksi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Natalie Naccache for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Laxmi Parekh has been running mental health workshops for Dubai’s migrant workers the past two years. Recently, she has incorporated the regional conflict into her lessons.

item.title: Quote of the Day:British Lab Hunts for Risks and Potential Catastrophes Lurking in A.I.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, June 3, 2026.

item.title: Pacific Palisades Voters Remain Frustrated by Fire Aftermath
item.description: Residents of the Pacific Palisades neighborhood of Los Angeles headed to the polls on Tuesday. Many are waiting for their homes to be rebuilt.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters in Pacific Palisades cast their ballots almost 18 months after much of their community was scorched by a wildfire.

item.title: U.S. Is Said to Be Investigating George Santos Over Kalshi Betting
item.description: The former congressman was referred to federal authorities after he bet on his own attendance at the State of the Union address, a person familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany and Sharon LaFraniere

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The inquiry into former Representative George Santos is the third insider trading case to come to light in recent weeks.

item.title: Spencer Pratt’s L.A. Mayor Campaign Is Fueled by Out-of-Town Cash
item.description: Mr. Pratt, a former reality TV star, has proved adept at raising funds among people who cannot vote for him. His rivals in the race have taken a more local approach.
item.dc:creator: Ken Bensinger

media:content.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Spencer Pratt, a candidate for Los Angeles mayor, campaigning last week.

item.title: In California Election Results, Beware the Red or Blue ‘Mirage’
item.description: Exactly when and how votes are counted in California can give misleading indications that a candidate is ahead.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People sorting through ballots at the Ballot Processing Center during a statewide special election in California last year.

item.title: Trump Endorses Abelardo De La Espriella, Right-Wing Presidential Candidate in Colombia
item.description: Mr. Trump said that the candidate, Abelardo De La Espriella, was important for the U.S.-Colombia relationship and called his left-wing rival a “Radical Left Marxist.”
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Abelardo De La Espriella at his closing rally in Medellín, Colombia. He gave campaign speeches from a bulletproof booth.

item.title: 9 Counties to Watch in the California Governor’s Race
item.description: The race might not be called on election night, but these counties could provide signals for what lies ahead.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Read Nick Bilton’s Letter to Scott Pelley
item.description: “Your antipathy to the future of the show has come through loud and clear,” Mr. Bilton said to the longtime CBS correspondent.

item.title: Radio Host With Trump’s Support Wins Republican House Primary in Montana
item.description: Aaron Flint defeated three other Republicans, including Christi Jacobsen, the Montana secretary of state.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: John Stember/Montana Free Press
media:content.media:description: Aaron Flint is aiming to replace Representative Ryan Zinke, who is not running for re-election.

item.title: Late-Arriving Ballots Could Lean Democratic in California
item.description: Democratic voters have been returning their ballots later than Republicans in this year’s primaries, which will make it difficult to interpret the results on election night.
item.dc:creator: Luke Vrotsos and Christine Zhang

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In recent years, more than 80 percent of ballots in California have been cast by mail. They’re sorted and processed in large facilities over a period of weeks.

item.title: Sanchez wins Democratic nod for New Mexico land commissioner
item.description: The tightly fought contest to control 9 million acres of state land revolved around which candidate could beat back President Trump and his public land chief, New Mexico’s Steve Pierce.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen

media:content.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Juan Sanchez, a candidate or Commissioner of Public Lands, in downtown Santa Fe, New Mexico.

item.title: Katie Porter: 5 Facts About the Candidate for California Governor
item.description: The former House member built her reputation by grilling corporate executives, and she has a strong command of policy. But questions about her demeanor have been a drag on her campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Katie Porter, a former member of Congress and the only woman among the top candidates for California governor, has demonstrated a strong command of policy. But her campaign has struggled to recover from viral videos that showed her berating an aide and a reporter.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘parsimonious’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 3 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 3, 2026
item.description: Jiahe Men makes his New York Times debut.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Isaac Brekken for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Stuck? Don’t call it a wash. Read the Wordplay column, and then keep solving.

item.title: Nithya Raman: 5 Facts About the Candidate for Los Angeles Mayor
item.description: Ms. Raman, a progressive Democrat, surprised many allies when she entered the Los Angeles mayor’s race at the last minute.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nithya Raman, a progressive member of the Los Angeles City Council, has drawn comparisons to New York’s mayor, Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: CBS News Fires Scott Pelley of ‘60 Minutes’
item.description: Mr. Pelley, a former “CBS Evening News” anchor, was ousted after months of tensions between staff and Bari Weiss, the network’s editor in chief.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:credit: Michele Crowe/CBS, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Scott Pelley was fired a day after a tense meeting where he accused the CBS editor in chief, Bari Weiss, of “murdering ‘60 Minutes.’”

item.title: Top Texas Election Official to Resign, Months Before General Contest
item.description: Jane Nelson, the Texas secretary of state, was appointed to the role overseeing statewide elections in 2023 by Gov. Greg Abbott.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Adam Powell/USA Today Network, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Jane Nelson, the top election official in Texas, did not give a reason for her departure.

item.title: Josh Turek, a Paralympian, Wins the Democratic Senate Primary in Iowa
item.description: Mr. Turek, a state lawmaker, beat Zach Wahls in a contest that tested the party’s anti-establishment fervor.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer

media:content.media:credit: KC McGinnis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Josh Turek, a self-described “prairie populist,” cast himself as a candidate who could sway independents and Republicans.

item.title: Voters in Los Angeles Say They ‘Don’t Have a Lot of Good Choices’
item.description: Many said they were struggling to choose from candidates they saw as uninspiring in both the mayor’s and governor’s races.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan and Maeve Reston

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters cast their primary ballots on Tuesday at the headquarters building of the Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority.

item.title: Adam Hamawy, a Progressive Democrat, Wins House Primary in New Jersey
item.description: Dr. Hamawy, a U.S. Army veteran, has been an outspoken critic of Israel’s war in Gaza. His victory makes him the heavy favorite to win in November in the left-leaning district.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully, Lauren Hard and Davaughnia Wilson

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dr. Adam Hamawy’s victory makes him the heavy favorite to win in November in New Jersey’s 12th Congressional District.

item.title: Deb Haaland wins nomination in New Mexico governor’s race
item.description: Ms. Haaland, a former Interior secretary, took a big step toward making history as the first Native American woman to be a governor when she won the primary in her Democratic state.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault

media:content.media:credit: Nina Riggio for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Deb Haaland, former secretary for the Interior Department, spoke to a group gathered at a Veterans Helping Veterans building in Gallup, New Mexico, in April.

item.title: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Republican-Friendly Map in Alabama
item.description: In the first major case since the justices narrowed the Voting Rights Act, the court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map preferred by Republicans that leaves the state with only one majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court ruled in 2023 that an Alabama congressional district map with only one majority-Black district diluted the power of Black voters.

item.title: California Election: Spencer Pratt Runs Second in L.A. Mayor’s Race
item.description: Mr. Pratt, a Republican reality TV star who lost his home in last year’s fires, could face Mayor Karen Bass in a runoff. The Trump-endorsed Steve Hilton and Xavier Becerra, the top polling Democrat, led early results for governor.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
